title: neonauclea gageana
intro: Neonauclea gageana, also known as the Gageana Neonauclea or Philippine Gagea, is a plant species native to the Philippines and is classified under the Rubiaceae family. It is a woody shrub or small tree that has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, primarily due to the presence of bioactive compounds such as alkaloids and phenolic acids. The plant has been used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, and has been reported to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Traditionally, the leaves and stems of Neonauclea gageana are prepared as herbal remedies, often in the form of infusions, decoctions, or ointments, which are used to treat various health conditions.
